task_completion.visual_evidence: A web browser is open and displaying the github.com homepage or repository pages.
A web browser is open and displaying the python.org homepage.

task_completion.description: Use browser Developer Tools to extract design data
task_completion.visual_evidence: The browser's Developer Tools (Elements/Inspector and Styles tabs) are open, actively inspecting page elements (headers, buttons, or body text) to view CSS properties like color, background-color, font-family, or font-size.

task_completion.description: Create the brand audit text file on the Desktop
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor is open with a file saved as 'brand_audit.txt' located in the /home/ga/Desktop/ directory, or the file is visible on the Desktop GUI.

task_completion.description: Record GitHub brand audit data in the report
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text file contains a distinct section for GitHub listing at least 3 hex color codes (e.g., #4078c0, #6cc644, #333333), a primary font family/stack, and font sizes for headers and body text.

task_completion.description: Record Python brand audit data in the report
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text file contains a distinct section for Python.org listing at least 3 hex color codes (e.g., #306998, #FFD43B), a primary font family/stack, and font sizes for headers and body text.

integrity.description: Agent used a graphical web browser to view the sites
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent interacted with a real web browser (like Edge, Chrome, or Firefox) rather than using terminal commands (like curl or wget) to fetch page contents.

integrity.description: Agent extracted data from the live sites rather than hallucinating
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent is seen using the browser's Developer Tools to find the exact hex codes and font sizes, rather than just typing known brand colors from memory without inspecting the page.

integrity.description: Agent manually compiled the report in a text editor
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent used a GUI text editor to write or paste the extracted information, rather than running a pre-written script to generate the text file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target sites are github.com and python.org. The final report must be saved to /home/ga/Desktop/brand_audit.txt. It must contain separate sections for each site. Each section must have: 1) At least 3 hex color codes (GitHub typically uses colors like #4078c0, #6cc644, #bd2c00, #333333; Python uses #306998, #FFD43B, #4B8BBE, etc.), 2) The primary font stack (e.g., GitHub uses a system font stack starting with -apple-system or BlinkMacSystemFont), and 3) Font sizes for both headers and body text. The agent is expected to use the browser's Developer Tools to find this exact CSS data.
